name: Wazuh Alert Jira Tickets - P1 P2 P3

nodes.name: Collect Wazuh Alerts
nodes.notes: Queries wazuh-alerts-* and stores grouped P1/P2/P3 alerts in /home/node/.n8n/output/alert-jira-triage-latest.json.

nodes.name: ChatGPT SOC Analysis
nodes.notes: Visible AI stage. If AI_ENABLE_ANALYSIS=true, this calls OpenAI/ChatGPT with Wazuh evidence and writes the mitigation analysis back into the latest alert triage JSON.

nodes.name: Create Jira Tickets
nodes.notes: Creates or deduplicates Jira tickets from the latest enriched alert triage JSON. If ChatGPT generated analysis, it is embedded in the Jira description.

nodes.name: Telegram P1/P2 Alert
nodes.notes: Sends Telegram notifications for P1/P2 alerts when TELEGRAM_ENABLE_ALERTS=true. Uses TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID from .env.

nodes.name: Jira Ticket Links
nodes.notes: Collects direct Jira URLs returned by the ticket creation stage.

parameters.content:
## Wazuh Alert Jira Tickets

1. Collect Wazuh P1/P2/P3 alerts.
2. Parse grouped incidents.
3. Run `ChatGPT SOC Analysis` as a visible AI stage.
4. Create or deduplicate Jira tickets.
5. Send Telegram for P1/P2 when enabled.
6. Return direct Jira links.

`ChatGPT SOC Analysis` calls OpenAI only when `AI_ENABLE_ANALYSIS=true` and `OPENAI_API_KEY` is configured. `Telegram P1/P2 Alert` sends messages only when `TELEGRAM_ENABLE_ALERTS=true`.
